AIVR 2026
Kobe, Japan | July 11-13
2026 10th International Conference on Artificial 
						Intelligence and Virtual Reality
ABOUT AIVR
2026 10th International Conference on Artificial 
						Intelligence and Virtual Reality (AIVR2026)
brings together researchers and scientists, both 
						industrial and academic, developing novel Artificial 
						Intelligence and Virtual Reality outcomes. Research in 
						Virtual Reality (VR) is concerned with computing 
						technologies that allow humans to see, hear, talk, 
						think, learn, and solve problems in virtual and 
						augmented environments. Research in Artificial 
						Intelligence (AI) addresses technologies that allow 
						computing machines to mimic these same human abilities. 
						Although these two fields evolved separately, they share 
						an interest in human senses, skills, and knowledge 
						production. Thus, bringing them together will enable us 
						to create more natural and realistic virtual worlds and 
						develop better, more effective applications. Ultimately, 
						this will lead to a future in which interaction between 
						man and machine are made naturally in virtual worlds, 
						with use cases and benefits we are only just beginning 
						to imagine.
AIVR 2026 
						will be held in
Kobe, Japan
during
July 11-13, 2026
with focusing AI technologies and virtual reality. Past 
						conferences were held in Nagoya (2018), Singapore 
						(2019), virtual conferences (2020, 2021 and 2022), 
						Kumamoto (2023), Fukuoka (2024) and Osaka (2025) respectively. This 
						conference in the successful AIVR series provides an 
						ideal opportunity for reflection on developments over 
						the last two decades and to focus on future 
						developments. We look forward to welcoming AIVR2027 in 
						Kobe to provide the international community with a 
						highly interactive and scientifically excellent 
						conference to further the translation of promising AI & 
						VR technologies.
SPONSORED BY
SUPPORTED BY
CONFERENCE TRACKS
Track 1:Systems, including techniques, performance, and implementation
Track 2:Content creation and modelling
Track 3:AI technologies
Track 4:Cognitive aspects, perception, user behaviour
Track 5:Interactions / interactive and responsive environments
Track 6:Applications and use cases
KEY DATES
Deadline for Paper Submission | May 20, 2026
Notification of Acceptance | June 10, 2026
Deadline for Registration and Camera Ready 
							Submission | June 20, 2026
Dates of the Conference | July 11-13, 2026
SUBMISSSION
Each paper must be submitted via the
Online Submission System
after being prepared by following the Springer 
							Template Specific Template (
LaTeX 
							Template
or
Microsoft Word Template
) and be subject to both 
							review and editing.
Call for Papers (PDF)
PUBLICATION
All accepted papers will appear in the conference proceedings published by Springer-Verlag in One volume of
Springer Book Series Smart Innovation, System and Technologies (SIST)
.The book series is indexed by SCOPUS, EI Compendex, INSPEC, WTI Frankfurt eG, zbMATH, Japanese Science and Technology Agency (JST), SCImago, 
DBLP. All books published in the series are submitted for consideration in Web of Science.

Kobe opened as Japan芒聙聶s first international trading port in 1868, and since then has developed into an international city that has adopted Western customs and culture.

Travel Information
茫聙聙
General Information
Kobe is a beautiful port city in western Japan with a population of 1.5 million, located less than an
                hour by train from Osaka and Kyoto.
Kobe opened as Japan芒聙聶s first international trading port in 1868, and since then has developed into an
                international city that has adopted Western customs and culture.
Kobe tourism is characterized by its compact cityscape and cosmopolitan environment cultivated by its
                history.
The downtown area stretches approximately 2 km from north to south, between the sea and the mountains.
                Within this area, there are beautifully landscaped waterfront areas such as Meriken Park & Harbor
                Land, the Kitano area with its European-style houses, and Nankinmachi Chinatown, and many shopping and
                dining spots.
A short distance away, you can enjoy the rich nature of Mt. Rokko and Mt. Maya, Japan芒聙聶s largest sake
                production area (Nada Gogo), and one of the most famous hot spring resorts in Japan (Arima Onsen).
And of course, don芒聙聶t forget to try the world-famous Kobe Beef!茫聙聙
Access
>
                Access to Kobe is really simple and convenient. It is within an hour from Kyoto and Osaka, as well as
                the major regional airport, Kansai International Airport (KIX), and transport options include trains,
                buses, and also the Shinkansen (bullet train).
We recommend you to stay and take enough time to enjoy everything in Kobe, but transport access is so
                convenient that you can make a day trip from Osaka or Kyoto.
Access / Official Travel Guide of KOBE
(For more details about Kobe, please visit
https://www.feel-kobe.jp/en/access/
)

Submission
Papers must be unpublished and must not 
									be submitted for publication elsewhere. All 
									papers will be reviewed by Program Committee 
									members and other experts active in the 
									field to ensure high quality and relevance 
									to the conference. Submissions should 
									include author information, abstract, 5-10 
									keywords, and be in PDF format. Paper length 
									should be longer than 10 pages in Springer 
									style format. It is imperative to prepare the paper 
									using the standard template (
LaTeX Template
or
Microsoft Word Template
).
Submission Method
Online submission system
or via email
aivr@chairmen.org
in PDF format.
All papers must explicitly describe their 
									relation to artificial intelligence, virtual 
									reality or their applications. All papers to 
									be submitted should be written in English 
									and formatted in the Springer-Verlag book 
									format. Please refer to the AIVR2026 and 
									Springer-Verlag web sites for directions on 
									the Paper Format which must be strictly 
									followed.
1. Each submission must be at least ten (10) 
									full pages. One regular registration can 
									cover a paper within Fifteen (15) pages, 
									including all figures, tables, and 
									references, Extra pages will be charged.
2. The full papers must be written in 
									English. All the tables and figures in the 
									paper should be original and un-published, 
									or authors must submit "Permission Request 
									Form"(
click to download
) for publication 
									purpose.聽 (If third-party materials are 
									used in your final paper, it is required by 
									Springer that authors should obtain 
									permission from third-party. Learn about 
									Third-Party Permissions:
https://www.springernature.com/gp/policies/editorial-policies/third-party-permissions
.
3. The full paper submission is required for 
									publication.
4. The version of your submission must be in 
									the requested format. The authors are 
									suggested to use the word template.
5. Please email aivr@chairmen.org if you 
									have any queries.
Plagiarism
Papers will be reviewed on the basis that 
									they do not contain plagiarized material and 
									have not been submitted to any other 
									conference at the same time (double 
									submission). These matters are taken very 
									seriously and the publisher will take action 
									against any author who has engaged in either 
									practice.
Review 
									process
AIVR welcomes the submission of application 
									papers with preference to the topics listed 
									in the call for papers. All submitted papers 
									will undergo a thorough review process; and 
									the peer review will be based on relevance, 
									originality, significance, quality and 
									clarity. AIVR follows a double-blind review 
									process. Authors are required to include 
									their names and affiliations in their papers 
									as illustrated in the sample templates. Your 
									PDF file must not include paper id, page 
									number & conference name.

Kobe, Japan | July 11-13
2026 10th International Conference on Artificial 
						Intelligence and Virtual Reality
ABOUT AIVR
2026 10th International Conference on Artificial 
						Intelligence and Virtual Reality (AIVR2026)
brings together researchers and scientists, both 
						industrial and academic, developing novel Artificial 
						Intelligence and Virtual Reality outcomes. Research in 
						Virtual Reality (VR) is concerned with computing 
						technologies that allow humans to see, hear, talk, 
						think, learn, and solve problems in virtual and 
						augmented environments. Research in Artificial 
						Intelligence (AI) addresses technologies that allow 
						computing machines to mimic these same human abilities. 
						Although these two fields evolved separately, they share 
						an interest in human senses, skills, and knowledge 
						production. Thus, bringing them together will enable us 
						to create more natural and realistic virtual worlds and 
						develop better, more effective applications. Ultimately, 
						this will lead to a future in which interaction between 
						man and machine are made naturally in virtual worlds, 
						with use cases and benefits we are only just beginning 
						to imagine.
AIVR 2026 
						will be held in
Kobe, Japan
during
July 11-13, 2026
with focusing AI technologies and virtual reality. Past 
						conferences were held in Nagoya (2018), Singapore 
						(2019), virtual conferences (2020, 2021 and 2022), 
						Kumamoto (2023), Fukuoka (2024) and Osaka (2025) respectively. This 
						conference in the successful AIVR series provides an 
						ideal opportunity for reflection on developments over 
						the last two decades and to focus on future 
						developments. We look forward to welcoming AIVR2027 in 
						Kobe to provide the international community with a 
						highly interactive and scientifically excellent 
						conference to further the translation of promising AI & 
						VR technologies.
SPONSORED BY
SUPPORTED BY
CONFERENCE TRACKS
Track 1:Systems, including techniques, performance, and implementation
Track 2:Content creation and modelling
Track 3:AI technologies
Track 4:Cognitive aspects, perception, user behaviour
Track 5:Interactions / interactive and responsive environments
Track 6:Applications and use cases
KEY DATES
Deadline for Paper Submission | May 20, 2026
Notification of Acceptance | June 10, 2026
Deadline for Registration and Camera Ready 
							Submission | June 20, 2026
Dates of the Conference | July 11-13, 2026
SUBMISSSION
Each paper must be submitted via the
Online Submission System
after being prepared by following the Springer 
							Template Specific Template (
LaTeX 
							Template
or
Microsoft Word Template
) and be subject to both 
							review and editing.
Call for Papers (PDF)
PUBLICATION

Kobe, Japan | July 11-13
2026 10th International Conference on Artificial 
						Intelligence and Virtual Reality
ABOUT AIVR
2026 10th International Conference on Artificial 
						Intelligence and Virtual Reality (AIVR2026)
brings together researchers and scientists, both 
						industrial and academic, developing novel Artificial 
						Intelligence and Virtual Reality outcomes. Research in 
						Virtual Reality (VR) is concerned with computing 
						technologies that allow humans to see, hear, talk, 
						think, learn, and solve problems in virtual and 
						augmented environments. Research in Artificial 
						Intelligence (AI) addresses technologies that allow 
						computing machines to mimic these same human abilities. 
						Although these two fields evolved separately, they share 
						an interest in human senses, skills, and knowledge 
						production. Thus, bringing them together will enable us 
						to create more natural and realistic virtual worlds and 
						develop better, more effective applications. Ultimately, 
						this will lead to a future in which interaction between 
						man and machine are made naturally in virtual worlds, 
						with use cases and benefits we are only just beginning 
						to imagine.
AIVR 2026 
						will be held in
Kobe, Japan
during
July 11-13, 2026
with focusing AI technologies and virtual reality. Past 
						conferences were held in Nagoya (2018), Singapore 
						(2019), virtual conferences (2020, 2021 and 2022), 
						Kumamoto (2023), Fukuoka (2024) and Osaka (2025) respectively. This 
						conference in the successful AIVR series provides an 
						ideal opportunity for reflection on developments over 
						the last two decades and to focus on future 
						developments. We look forward to welcoming AIVR2027 in 
						Kobe to provide the international community with a 
						highly interactive and scientifically excellent 
						conference to further the translation of promising AI & 
						VR technologies.

Submission
Papers must be unpublished and must not 
									be submitted for publication elsewhere. All 
									papers will be reviewed by Program Committee 
									members and other experts active in the 
									field to ensure high quality and relevance 
									to the conference. Submissions should 
									include author information, abstract, 5-10 
									keywords, and be in PDF format. Paper length 
									should be longer than 10 pages in Springer 
									style format. It is imperative to prepare the paper 
									using the standard template (
LaTeX Template
or
Microsoft Word Template
).
Submission Method
Online submission system
or via email
aivr@chairmen.org
in PDF format.
All papers must explicitly describe their 
									relation to artificial intelligence, virtual 
									reality or their applications. All papers to 
									be submitted should be written in English 
									and formatted in the Springer-Verlag book 
									format. Please refer to the AIVR2026 and 
									Springer-Verlag web sites for directions on 
									the Paper Format which must be strictly 
									followed.
1. Each submission must be at least ten (10) 
									full pages. One regular registration can 
									cover a paper within Fifteen (15) pages, 
									including all figures, tables, and 
									references, Extra pages will be charged.
2. The full papers must be written in 
									English. All the tables and figures in the 
									paper should be original and un-published, 
									or authors must submit "Permission Request 
									Form"(
click to download
) for publication 
									purpose.聽 (If third-party materials are 
									used in your final paper, it is required by 
									Springer that authors should obtain 
									permission from third-party. Learn about 
									Third-Party Permissions:
https://www.springernature.com/gp/policies/editorial-policies/third-party-permissions
.
3. The full paper submission is required for 
									publication.
4. The version of your submission must be in 
									the requested format. The authors are 
									suggested to use the word template.
5. Please email aivr@chairmen.org if you 
									have any queries.
Plagiarism
Papers will be reviewed on the basis that 
									they do not contain plagiarized material and 
									have not been submitted to any other 
									conference at the same time (double 
									submission). These matters are taken very 
									seriously and the publisher will take action 
									against any author who has engaged in either 
									practice.
Review 
									process
AIVR welcomes the submission of application 
									papers with preference to the topics listed 
									in the call for papers. All submitted papers 
									will undergo a thorough review process; and 
									the peer review will be based on relevance, 
									originality, significance, quality and 
									clarity. AIVR follows a double-blind review 
									process. Authors are required to include 
									their names and affiliations in their papers 
									as illustrated in the sample templates. Your 
									PDF file must not include paper id, page 
									number & conference name.

Travel Information
茫聙聙
General Information
Kobe is a beautiful port city in western Japan with a population of 1.5 million, located less than an
                hour by train from Osaka and Kyoto.
Kobe opened as Japan芒聙聶s first international trading port in 1868, and since then has developed into an
                international city that has adopted Western customs and culture.
Kobe tourism is characterized by its compact cityscape and cosmopolitan environment cultivated by its
                history.
The downtown area stretches approximately 2 km from north to south, between the sea and the mountains.
                Within this area, there are beautifully landscaped waterfront areas such as Meriken Park & Harbor
                Land, the Kitano area with its European-style houses, and Nankinmachi Chinatown, and many shopping and
                dining spots.
A short distance away, you can enjoy the rich nature of Mt. Rokko and Mt. Maya, Japan芒聙聶s largest sake
                production area (Nada Gogo), and one of the most famous hot spring resorts in Japan (Arima Onsen).
And of course, don芒聙聶t forget to try the world-famous Kobe Beef!茫聙聙
Access
>
                Access to Kobe is really simple and convenient. It is within an hour from Kyoto and Osaka, as well as
                the major regional airport, Kansai International Airport (KIX), and transport options include trains,
                buses, and also the Shinkansen (bullet train).
We recommend you to stay and take enough time to enjoy everything in Kobe, but transport access is so
                convenient that you can make a day trip from Osaka or Kyoto.
Access / Official Travel Guide of KOBE
(For more details about Kobe, please visit
https://www.feel-kobe.jp/en/access/

2026 10th International Conference on Artificial 
						Intelligence and Virtual Reality
ABOUT AIVR
2026 10th International Conference on Artificial 
						Intelligence and Virtual Reality (AIVR2026)
brings together researchers and scientists, both 
						industrial and academic, developing novel Artificial 
						Intelligence and Virtual Reality outcomes. Research in 
						Virtual Reality (VR) is concerned with computing 
						technologies that allow humans to see, hear, talk, 
						think, learn, and solve problems in virtual and 
						augmented environments. Research in Artificial 
						Intelligence (AI) addresses technologies that allow 
						computing machines to mimic these same human abilities. 
						Although these two fields evolved separately, they share 
						an interest in human senses, skills, and knowledge 
						production. Thus, bringing them together will enable us 
						to create more natural and realistic virtual worlds and 
						develop better, more effective applications. Ultimately, 
						this will lead to a future in which interaction between 
						man and machine are made naturally in virtual worlds, 
						with use cases and benefits we are only just beginning 
						to imagine.
AIVR 2026 
						will be held in
Kobe, Japan
during
July 11-13, 2026
with focusing AI technologies and virtual reality. Past 
						conferences were held in Nagoya (2018), Singapore 
						(2019), virtual conferences (2020, 2021 and 2022), 
						Kumamoto (2023), Fukuoka (2024) and Osaka (2025) respectively. This 
						conference in the successful AIVR series provides an 
						ideal opportunity for reflection on developments over 
						the last two decades and to focus on future 
						developments. We look forward to welcoming AIVR2027 in 
						Kobe to provide the international community with a 
						highly interactive and scientifically excellent 
						conference to further the translation of promising AI & 
						VR technologies.
SPONSORED BY
SUPPORTED BY
CONFERENCE TRACKS
Track 1:Systems, including techniques, performance, and implementation
Track 2:Content creation and modelling
Track 3:AI technologies
Track 4:Cognitive aspects, perception, user behaviour
Track 5:Interactions / interactive and responsive environments
Track 6:Applications and use cases
KEY DATES
Deadline for Paper Submission | May 20, 2026
Notification of Acceptance | June 10, 2026
Deadline for Registration and Camera Ready 
							Submission | June 20, 2026
Dates of the Conference | July 11-13, 2026

Each paper must be submitted via the
Online Submission System
after being prepared by following the Springer 
							Template Specific Template (
LaTeX 
							Template
or
Microsoft Word Template
) and be subject to both 
							review and editing.
Call for Papers (PDF)
PUBLICATION
All accepted papers will appear in the conference proceedings published by Springer-Verlag in One volume of
Springer Book Series Smart Innovation, System and Technologies (SIST)
.The book series is indexed by SCOPUS, EI Compendex, INSPEC, WTI Frankfurt eG, zbMATH, Japanese Science and Technology Agency (JST), SCImago, 
DBLP. All books published in the series are submitted for consideration in Web of Science.

Submission
Papers must be unpublished and must not 
									be submitted for publication elsewhere. All 
									papers will be reviewed by Program Committee 
									members and other experts active in the 
									field to ensure high quality and relevance 
									to the conference. Submissions should 
									include author information, abstract, 5-10 
									keywords, and be in PDF format. Paper length 
									should be longer than 10 pages in Springer 
									style format. It is imperative to prepare the paper 
									using the standard template (
LaTeX Template
or
Microsoft Word Template
).
Submission Method
Online submission system
or via email
aivr@chairmen.org
in PDF format.
All papers must explicitly describe their 
									relation to artificial intelligence, virtual 
									reality or their applications. All papers to 
									be submitted should be written in English 
									and formatted in the Springer-Verlag book 
									format. Please refer to the AIVR2026 and 
									Springer-Verlag web sites for directions on 
									the Paper Format which must be strictly 
									followed.
1. Each submission must be at least ten (10) 
									full pages. One regular registration can 
									cover a paper within Fifteen (15) pages, 
									including all figures, tables, and 
									references, Extra pages will be charged.
2. The full papers must be written in 
									English. All the tables and figures in the 
									paper should be original and un-published, 
									or authors must submit "Permission Request 
									Form"(
click to download
) for publication 
									purpose.聽 (If third-party materials are 
									used in your final paper, it is required by 
									Springer that authors should obtain 
									permission from third-party. Learn about 
									Third-Party Permissions:
https://www.springernature.com/gp/policies/editorial-policies/third-party-permissions
.
3. The full paper submission is required for 
									publication.
4. The version of your submission must be in 
									the requested format. The authors are 
									suggested to use the word template.
5. Please email aivr@chairmen.org if you 
									have any queries.
Plagiarism
Papers will be reviewed on the basis that 
									they do not contain plagiarized material and 
									have not been submitted to any other 
									conference at the same time (double 
									submission). These matters are taken very 
									seriously and the publisher will take action 
									against any author who has engaged in either 
									practice.
Review 
									process
AIVR welcomes the submission of application 
									papers with preference to the topics listed 
									in the call for papers. All submitted papers 
									will undergo a thorough review process; and 
									the peer review will be based on relevance, 
									originality, significance, quality and 
									clarity. AIVR follows a double-blind review 
									process. Authors are required to include 
									their names and affiliations in their papers 
									as illustrated in the sample templates. Your 
									PDF file must not include paper id, page 
									number & conference name.

Kobe, Japan | July 11-13
2026 10th International Conference on Artificial 
						Intelligence and Virtual Reality
ABOUT AIVR
2026 10th International Conference on Artificial 
						Intelligence and Virtual Reality (AIVR2026)
brings together researchers and scientists, both 
						industrial and academic, developing novel Artificial 
						Intelligence and Virtual Reality outcomes. Research in 
						Virtual Reality (VR) is concerned with computing 
						technologies that allow humans to see, hear, talk, 
						think, learn, and solve problems in virtual and 
						augmented environments. Research in Artificial 
						Intelligence (AI) addresses technologies that allow 
						computing machines to mimic these same human abilities. 
						Although these two fields evolved separately, they share 
						an interest in human senses, skills, and knowledge 
						production. Thus, bringing them together will enable us 
						to create more natural and realistic virtual worlds and 
						develop better, more effective applications. Ultimately, 
						this will lead to a future in which interaction between 
						man and machine are made naturally in virtual worlds, 
						with use cases and benefits we are only just beginning 
						to imagine.
AIVR 2026 
						will be held in
Kobe, Japan
during
July 11-13, 2026
with focusing AI technologies and virtual reality. Past 
						conferences were held in Nagoya (2018), Singapore 
						(2019), virtual conferences (2020, 2021 and 2022), 
						Kumamoto (2023), Fukuoka (2024) and Osaka (2025) respectively. This 
						conference in the successful AIVR series provides an 
						ideal opportunity for reflection on developments over 
						the last two decades and to focus on future 
						developments. We look forward to welcoming AIVR2027 in 
						Kobe to provide the international community with a 
						highly interactive and scientifically excellent 
						conference to further the translation of promising AI & 
						VR technologies.
SPONSORED BY
SUPPORTED BY
CONFERENCE TRACKS
Track 1:Systems, including techniques, performance, and implementation
Track 2:Content creation and modelling
Track 3:AI technologies
Track 4:Cognitive aspects, perception, user behaviour
Track 5:Interactions / interactive and responsive environments
Track 6:Applications and use cases

Travel Information
茫聙聙
General Information
Kobe is a beautiful port city in western Japan with a population of 1.5 million, located less than an
                hour by train from Osaka and Kyoto.
Kobe opened as Japan芒聙聶s first international trading port in 1868, and since then has developed into an
                international city that has adopted Western customs and culture.
Kobe tourism is characterized by its compact cityscape and cosmopolitan environment cultivated by its
                history.
The downtown area stretches approximately 2 km from north to south, between the sea and the mountains.
                Within this area, there are beautifully landscaped waterfront areas such as Meriken Park & Harbor
                Land, the Kitano area with its European-style houses, and Nankinmachi Chinatown, and many shopping and
                dining spots.
A short distance away, you can enjoy the rich nature of Mt. Rokko and Mt. Maya, Japan芒聙聶s largest sake
                production area (Nada Gogo), and one of the most famous hot spring resorts in Japan (Arima Onsen).
And of course, don芒聙聶t forget to try the world-famous Kobe Beef!茫聙聙
Access
>
                Access to Kobe is really simple and convenient. It is within an hour from Kyoto and Osaka, as well as
                the major regional airport, Kansai International Airport (KIX), and transport options include trains,
                buses, and also the Shinkansen (bullet train).
We recommend you to stay and take enough time to enjoy everything in Kobe, but transport access is so
                convenient that you can make a day trip from Osaka or Kyoto.
Access / Official Travel Guide of KOBE
(For more details about Kobe, please visit
https://www.feel-kobe.jp/en/access/
)

Kobe, Japan | July 11-13
2026 10th International Conference on Artificial 
						Intelligence and Virtual Reality
ABOUT AIVR
2026 10th International Conference on Artificial 
						Intelligence and Virtual Reality (AIVR2026)
brings together researchers and scientists, both 
						industrial and academic, developing novel Artificial 
						Intelligence and Virtual Reality outcomes. Research in 
						Virtual Reality (VR) is concerned with computing 
						technologies that allow humans to see, hear, talk, 
						think, learn, and solve problems in virtual and 
						augmented environments. Research in Artificial 
						Intelligence (AI) addresses technologies that allow 
						computing machines to mimic these same human abilities. 
						Although these two fields evolved separately, they share 
						an interest in human senses, skills, and knowledge 
						production. Thus, bringing them together will enable us 
						to create more natural and realistic virtual worlds and 
						develop better, more effective applications. Ultimately, 
						this will lead to a future in which interaction between 
						man and machine are made naturally in virtual worlds, 
						with use cases and benefits we are only just beginning 
						to imagine.
AIVR 2026 
						will be held in
Kobe, Japan
during
July 11-13, 2026
with focusing AI technologies and virtual reality. Past 
						conferences were held in Nagoya (2018), Singapore 
						(2019), virtual conferences (2020, 2021 and 2022), 
						Kumamoto (2023), Fukuoka (2024) and Osaka (2025) respectively. This 
						conference in the successful AIVR series provides an 
						ideal opportunity for reflection on developments over 
						the last two decades and to focus on future 
						developments. We look forward to welcoming AIVR2027 in 
						Kobe to provide the international community with a 
						highly interactive and scientifically excellent 
						conference to further the translation of promising AI & 
						VR technologies.
SPONSORED BY
SUPPORTED BY
CONFERENCE TRACKS
Track 1:Systems, including techniques, performance, and implementation
Track 2:Content creation and modelling
Track 3:AI technologies
Track 4:Cognitive aspects, perception, user behaviour
Track 5:Interactions / interactive and responsive environments
Track 6:Applications and use cases
KEY DATES
Deadline for Paper Submission | May 20, 2026
Notification of Acceptance | June 10, 2026
Deadline for Registration and Camera Ready 
							Submission | June 20, 2026
Dates of the Conference | July 11-13, 2026
SUBMISSSION
Each paper must be submitted via the
Online Submission System
after being prepared by following the Springer 
							Template Specific Template (
LaTeX 
							Template
or
Microsoft Word Template
) and be subject to both 
							review and editing.
Call for Papers (PDF)
PUBLICATION
All accepted papers will appear in the conference proceedings published by Springer-Verlag in One volume of
Springer Book Series Smart Innovation, System and Technologies (SIST)
.The book series is indexed by SCOPUS, EI Compendex, INSPEC, WTI Frankfurt eG, zbMATH, Japanese Science and Technology Agency (JST), SCImago, 
DBLP. All books published in the series are submitted for consideration in Web of Science.

About Kobe
Kobe is a beautiful port city in western Japan with a population of 1.5 million, located less than an hour by train from Osaka and Kyoto. Kobe opened as Japan芒聙聶s first international trading port in 1868, and since then has developed into an international city that has adopted Western customs and culture.

Submission
Papers must be unpublished and must not 
									be submitted for publication elsewhere. All 
									papers will be reviewed by Program Committee 
									members and other experts active in the 
									field to ensure high quality and relevance 
									to the conference. Submissions should 
									include author information, abstract, 5-10 
									keywords, and be in PDF format. Paper length 
									should be longer than 10 pages in Springer 
									style format. It is imperative to prepare the paper 
									using the standard template (
LaTeX Template
or
Microsoft Word Template
).
Submission Method
Online submission system
or via email
aivr@chairmen.org
in PDF format.
All papers must explicitly describe their 
									relation to artificial intelligence, virtual 
									reality or their applications. All papers to 
									be submitted should be written in English 
									and formatted in the Springer-Verlag book 
									format. Please refer to the AIVR2026 and 
									Springer-Verlag web sites for directions on 
									the Paper Format which must be strictly 
									followed.
1. Each submission must be at least ten (10) 
									full pages. One regular registration can 
									cover a paper within Fifteen (15) pages, 
									including all figures, tables, and 
									references, Extra pages will be charged.
2. The full papers must be written in 
									English. All the tables and figures in the 
									paper should be original and un-published, 
									or authors must submit "Permission Request 
									Form"(
click to download
) for publication 
									purpose.聽 (If third-party materials are 
									used in your final paper, it is required by 
									Springer that authors should obtain 
									permission from third-party. Learn about 
									Third-Party Permissions:
https://www.springernature.com/gp/policies/editorial-policies/third-party-permissions
.
3. The full paper submission is required for 
									publication.
4. The version of your submission must be in 
									the requested format. The authors are 
									suggested to use the word template.
5. Please email aivr@chairmen.org if you 
									have any queries.
Plagiarism
Papers will be reviewed on the basis that 
									they do not contain plagiarized material and 
									have not been submitted to any other 
									conference at the same time (double 
									submission). These matters are taken very 
									seriously and the publisher will take action 
									against any author who has engaged in either 
									practice.
Review 
									process
AIVR welcomes the submission of application 
									papers with preference to the topics listed 
									in the call for papers. All submitted papers 
									will undergo a thorough review process; and 
									the peer review will be based on relevance, 
									originality, significance, quality and 
									clarity. AIVR follows a double-blind review 
									process. Authors are required to include 
									their names and affiliations in their papers 
									as illustrated in the sample templates. Your 
									PDF file must not include paper id, page 
									number & conference name.

Travel Information
茫聙聙
General Information
Kobe is a beautiful port city in western Japan with a population of 1.5 million, located less than an
                hour by train from Osaka and Kyoto.
Kobe opened as Japan芒聙聶s first international trading port in 1868, and since then has developed into an
                international city that has adopted Western customs and culture.
Kobe tourism is characterized by its compact cityscape and cosmopolitan environment cultivated by its
                history.
The downtown area stretches approximately 2 km from north to south, between the sea and the mountains.
                Within this area, there are beautifully landscaped waterfront areas such as Meriken Park & Harbor
                Land, the Kitano area with its European-style houses, and Nankinmachi Chinatown, and many shopping and
                dining spots.
A short distance away, you can enjoy the rich nature of Mt. Rokko and Mt. Maya, Japan芒聙聶s largest sake
                production area (Nada Gogo), and one of the most famous hot spring resorts in Japan (Arima Onsen).
And of course, don芒聙聶t forget to try the world-famous Kobe Beef!茫聙聙
Access
>
                Access to Kobe is really simple and convenient. It is within an hour from Kyoto and Osaka, as well as
                the major regional airport, Kansai International Airport (KIX), and transport options include trains,
                buses, and also the Shinkansen (bullet train).
We recommend you to stay and take enough time to enjoy everything in Kobe, but transport access is so
                convenient that you can make a day trip from Osaka or Kyoto.
Access / Official Travel Guide of KOBE
(For more details about Kobe, please visit
https://www.feel-kobe.jp/en/access/
)

Kobe, Japan | July 11-13
2026 10th International Conference on Artificial 
						Intelligence and Virtual Reality
ABOUT AIVR
2026 10th International Conference on Artificial 
						Intelligence and Virtual Reality (AIVR2026)
brings together researchers and scientists, both 
						industrial and academic, developing novel Artificial 
						Intelligence and Virtual Reality outcomes. Research in 
						Virtual Reality (VR) is concerned with computing 
						technologies that allow humans to see, hear, talk, 
						think, learn, and solve problems in virtual and 
						augmented environments. Research in Artificial 
						Intelligence (AI) addresses technologies that allow 
						computing machines to mimic these same human abilities. 
						Although these two fields evolved separately, they share 
						an interest in human senses, skills, and knowledge 
						production. Thus, bringing them together will enable us 
						to create more natural and realistic virtual worlds and 
						develop better, more effective applications. Ultimately, 
						this will lead to a future in which interaction between 
						man and machine are made naturally in virtual worlds, 
						with use cases and benefits we are only just beginning 
						to imagine.
AIVR 2026 
						will be held in
Kobe, Japan
during
July 11-13, 2026
with focusing AI technologies and virtual reality. Past 
						conferences were held in Nagoya (2018), Singapore 
						(2019), virtual conferences (2020, 2021 and 2022), 
						Kumamoto (2023), Fukuoka (2024) and Osaka (2025) respectively. This 
						conference in the successful AIVR series provides an 
						ideal opportunity for reflection on developments over 
						the last two decades and to focus on future 
						developments. We look forward to welcoming AIVR2027 in 
						Kobe to provide the international community with a 
						highly interactive and scientifically excellent 
						conference to further the translation of promising AI & 
						VR technologies.
SPONSORED BY
SUPPORTED BY
CONFERENCE TRACKS
Track 1:Systems, including techniques, performance, and implementation
Track 2:Content creation and modelling
Track 3:AI technologies
Track 4:Cognitive aspects, perception, user behaviour
Track 5:Interactions / interactive and responsive environments
Track 6:Applications and use cases
KEY DATES
Deadline for Paper Submission | May 20, 2026
Notification of Acceptance | June 10, 2026
Deadline for Registration and Camera Ready 
							Submission | June 20, 2026
Dates of the Conference | July 11-13, 2026
SUBMISSSION
Each paper must be submitted via the
Online Submission System
after being prepared by following the Springer 
							Template Specific Template (
LaTeX 
							Template
or
Microsoft Word Template
) and be subject to both 
							review and editing.
Call for Papers (PDF)
PUBLICATION
All accepted papers will appear in the conference proceedings published by Springer-Verlag in One volume of
Springer Book Series Smart Innovation, System and Technologies (SIST)
.The book series is indexed by SCOPUS, EI Compendex, INSPEC, WTI Frankfurt eG, zbMATH, Japanese Science and Technology Agency (JST), SCImago, 
DBLP. All books published in the series are submitted for consideration in Web of Science.
AIVR2025 proceedings | Published by
Springer-SIST, Volume 463
| Indexed by EI Compendex
AIVR2024 proceedings | Published by
Springer-SIST, Volume 432
| Indexed by EI Compendex, Scopus
AIVR2023 proceedings | Published by
Springer-SIST, Volume 382
| Indexed by EI Compendex, Scopus
AIVR2022 proceedings | Published by
Springer-SIST, Volume 330
| Indexed by EI Compendex, Scopus
AIVR2021 proceedings | Published by
ACM ICPS
| Indexed by EI Compendex and Scopus.
AIVR2020 proceedings | Published by
ACM ICPS
| Indexed by EI Compendex and Scopus.
AIVR2019 proceedings | Published by
ACM ICPS
| Indexed by EI Compendex and Scopus.
AIVR2018 proceedings | Published by
ACM ICPS
| Indexed by EI Compendex and Scopus.
Extended 
and improved version of selected papers from AIVR2026 will be recommended to be 
published in
International 
Journal of Reasoning-based Intelligent Systems (ISSN online:1755-0564; ISSN 
print: 1755-0556)
, which will be submitted for indexing by Scopus 
(Elsevier), Compendex [formerly Ei] (Elsevier), Academic OneFile (Gale, 
cnpLINKer (CNPIEC), DBLP Computer Science Bibliography
AIVR 
							History
About Kobe
Kobe is a beautiful port city in western Japan with a population of 1.5 million, located less than an hour by train from Osaka and Kyoto. Kobe opened as Japan芒聙聶s first international trading port in 1868, and since then has developed into an international city that has adopted Western customs and culture.
Contact Us
Don't hesitate to contact us via
aivr@chairmen.org
should you have any query about the conference.

Submission
Papers must be unpublished and must not 
									be submitted for publication elsewhere. All 
									papers will be reviewed by Program Committee 
									members and other experts active in the 
									field to ensure high quality and relevance 
									to the conference. Submissions should 
									include author information, abstract, 5-10 
									keywords, and be in PDF format. Paper length 
									should be longer than 10 pages in Springer 
									style format. It is imperative to prepare the paper 
									using the standard template (
LaTeX Template
or
Microsoft Word Template
).
Submission Method
Online submission system
or via email
aivr@chairmen.org
in PDF format.
All papers must explicitly describe their 
									relation to artificial intelligence, virtual 
									reality or their applications. All papers to 
									be submitted should be written in English 
									and formatted in the Springer-Verlag book 
									format. Please refer to the AIVR2026 and 
									Springer-Verlag web sites for directions on 
									the Paper Format which must be strictly 
									followed.
1. Each submission must be at least ten (10) 
									full pages. One regular registration can 
									cover a paper within Fifteen (15) pages, 
									including all figures, tables, and 
									references, Extra pages will be charged.
2. The full papers must be written in 
									English. All the tables and figures in the 
									paper should be original and un-published, 
									or authors must submit "Permission Request 
									Form"(
click to download
) for publication 
									purpose.聽 (If third-party materials are 
									used in your final paper, it is required by 
									Springer that authors should obtain 
									permission from third-party. Learn about 
									Third-Party Permissions:
https://www.springernature.com/gp/policies/editorial-policies/third-party-permissions
.
3. The full paper submission is required for 
									publication.
4. The version of your submission must be in 
									the requested format. The authors are 
									suggested to use the word template.
5. Please email aivr@chairmen.org if you 
									have any queries.
Plagiarism
Papers will be reviewed on the basis that 
									they do not contain plagiarized material and 
									have not been submitted to any other 
									conference at the same time (double 
									submission). These matters are taken very 
									seriously and the publisher will take action 
									against any author who has engaged in either 
									practice.
Review 
									process
AIVR welcomes the submission of application 
									papers with preference to the topics listed 
									in the call for papers. All submitted papers 
									will undergo a thorough review process; and 
									the peer review will be based on relevance, 
									originality, significance, quality and 
									clarity. AIVR follows a double-blind review 
									process. Authors are required to include 
									their names and affiliations in their papers 
									as illustrated in the sample templates. Your 
									PDF file must not include paper id, page 
									number & conference name.

Travel Information
茫聙聙
General Information
Kobe is a beautiful port city in western Japan with a population of 1.5 million, located less than an
                hour by train from Osaka and Kyoto.
Kobe opened as Japan芒聙聶s first international trading port in 1868, and since then has developed into an
                international city that has adopted Western customs and culture.
Kobe tourism is characterized by its compact cityscape and cosmopolitan environment cultivated by its
                history.
The downtown area stretches approximately 2 km from north to south, between the sea and the mountains.
                Within this area, there are beautifully landscaped waterfront areas such as Meriken Park & Harbor
                Land, the Kitano area with its European-style houses, and Nankinmachi Chinatown, and many shopping and
                dining spots.
A short distance away, you can enjoy the rich nature of Mt. Rokko and Mt. Maya, Japan芒聙聶s largest sake
                production area (Nada Gogo), and one of the most famous hot spring resorts in Japan (Arima Onsen).
And of course, don芒聙聶t forget to try the world-famous Kobe Beef!茫聙聙
Access
>
                Access to Kobe is really simple and convenient. It is within an hour from Kyoto and Osaka, as well as
                the major regional airport, Kansai International Airport (KIX), and transport options include trains,
                buses, and also the Shinkansen (bullet train).
We recommend you to stay and take enough time to enjoy everything in Kobe, but transport access is so
                convenient that you can make a day trip from Osaka or Kyoto.
Access / Official Travel Guide of KOBE
(For more details about Kobe, please visit
https://www.feel-kobe.jp/en/access/
)

AIVR 2026
Kobe, Japan | July 11-13
2026 10th International Conference on Artificial 
						Intelligence and Virtual Reality
ABOUT AIVR
2026 10th International Conference on Artificial 
						Intelligence and Virtual Reality (AIVR2026)
brings together researchers and scientists, both 
						industrial and academic, developing novel Artificial 
						Intelligence and Virtual Reality outcomes. Research in 
						Virtual Reality (VR) is concerned with computing 
						technologies that allow humans to see, hear, talk, 
						think, learn, and solve problems in virtual and 
						augmented environments. Research in Artificial 
						Intelligence (AI) addresses technologies that allow 
						computing machines to mimic these same human abilities. 
						Although these two fields evolved separately, they share 
						an interest in human senses, skills, and knowledge 
						production. Thus, bringing them together will enable us 
						to create more natural and realistic virtual worlds and 
						develop better, more effective applications. Ultimately, 
						this will lead to a future in which interaction between 
						man and machine are made naturally in virtual worlds, 
						with use cases and benefits we are only just beginning 
						to imagine.
AIVR 2026 
						will be held in
Kobe, Japan
during
July 11-13, 2026
with focusing AI technologies and virtual reality. Past 
						conferences were held in Nagoya (2018), Singapore 
						(2019), virtual conferences (2020, 2021 and 2022), 
						Kumamoto (2023), Fukuoka (2024) and Osaka (2025) respectively. This 
						conference in the successful AIVR series provides an 
						ideal opportunity for reflection on developments over 
						the last two decades and to focus on future 
						developments. We look forward to welcoming AIVR2027 in 
						Kobe to provide the international community with a 
						highly interactive and scientifically excellent 
						conference to further the translation of promising AI & 
						VR technologies.
SPONSORED BY
SUPPORTED BY
CONFERENCE TRACKS
Track 1:Systems, including techniques, performance, and implementation
Track 2:Content creation and modelling
Track 3:AI technologies
Track 4:Cognitive aspects, perception, user behaviour
Track 5:Interactions / interactive and responsive environments
Track 6:Applications and use cases
KEY DATES
Deadline for Paper Submission | May 20, 2026
Notification of Acceptance | June 10, 2026
Deadline for Registration and Camera Ready 
							Submission | June 20, 2026
Dates of the Conference | July 11-13, 2026
SUBMISSSION
Each paper must be submitted via the
Online Submission System
after being prepared by following the Springer 
							Template Specific Template (
LaTeX 
							Template
or
Microsoft Word Template
) and be subject to both 
							review and editing.
Call for Papers (PDF)
PUBLICATION
All accepted papers will appear in the conference proceedings published by Springer-Verlag in One volume of
Springer Book Series Smart Innovation, System and Technologies (SIST)
.The book series is indexed by SCOPUS, EI Compendex, INSPEC, WTI Frankfurt eG, zbMATH, Japanese Science and Technology Agency (JST), SCImago, 
DBLP. All books published in the series are submitted for consideration in Web of Science.
AIVR2025 proceedings | Published by
Springer-SIST, Volume 463
| Indexed by EI Compendex
AIVR2024 proceedings | Published by
Springer-SIST, Volume 432
| Indexed by EI Compendex, Scopus

| Indexed by EI Compendex, Scopus

| Indexed by EI Compendex, Scopus

| Indexed by EI Compendex and Scopus.

AIVR2018 proceedings | Published by

| Indexed by EI Compendex and Scopus.
Extended 
and improved version of selected papers from AIVR2026 will be recommended to be 
published in
International 
Journal of Reasoning-based Intelligent Systems (ISSN online:1755-0564; ISSN 
print: 1755-0556)
, which will be submitted for indexing by Scopus 
(Elsevier), Compendex [formerly Ei] (Elsevier), Academic OneFile (Gale, 
cnpLINKer (CNPIEC), DBLP Computer Science Bibliography
AIVR 
							History
About Kobe
Kobe is a beautiful port city in western Japan with a population of 1.5 million, located less than an hour by train from Osaka and Kyoto. Kobe opened as Japan芒聙聶s first international trading port in 1868, and since then has developed into an international city that has adopted Western customs and culture.

should you have any query about the conference.

Submission
Papers must be unpublished and must not 
									be submitted for publication elsewhere. All 
									papers will be reviewed by Program Committee 
									members and other experts active in the 
									field to ensure high quality and relevance 
									to the conference. Submissions should 
									include author information, abstract, 5-10 
									keywords, and be in PDF format. Paper length 
									should be longer than 10 pages in Springer 
									style format. It is imperative to prepare the paper 
									using the standard template (
LaTeX Template
or
Microsoft Word Template
).
Submission Method
Online submission system
or via email
aivr@chairmen.org
in PDF format.
All papers must explicitly describe their 
									relation to artificial intelligence, virtual 
									reality or their applications. All papers to 
									be submitted should be written in English 
									and formatted in the Springer-Verlag book 
									format. Please refer to the AIVR2026 and 
									Springer-Verlag web sites for directions on 
									the Paper Format which must be strictly 
									followed.
1. Each submission must be at least ten (10) 
									full pages. One regular registration can 
									cover a paper within Fifteen (15) pages, 
									including all figures, tables, and 
									references, Extra pages will be charged.
2. The full papers must be written in 
									English. All the tables and figures in the 
									paper should be original and un-published, 
									or authors must submit "Permission Request 
									Form"(
click to download
) for publication 
									purpose.聽 (If third-party materials are 
									used in your final paper, it is required by 
									Springer that authors should obtain 
									permission from third-party. Learn about 
									Third-Party Permissions:
https://www.springernature.com/gp/policies/editorial-policies/third-party-permissions
.
3. The full paper submission is required for 
									publication.
4. The version of your submission must be in 
									the requested format. The authors are 
									suggested to use the word template.
5. Please email aivr@chairmen.org if you 
									have any queries.
Plagiarism
Papers will be reviewed on the basis that 
									they do not contain plagiarized material and 
									have not been submitted to any other 
									conference at the same time (double 
									submission). These matters are taken very 
									seriously and the publisher will take action 
									against any author who has engaged in either 
									practice.
Review 
									process
AIVR welcomes the submission of application 
									papers with preference to the topics listed 
									in the call for papers. All submitted papers 
									will undergo a thorough review process; and 
									the peer review will be based on relevance, 
									originality, significance, quality and 
									clarity. AIVR follows a double-blind review 
									process. Authors are required to include 
									their names and affiliations in their papers 
									as illustrated in the sample templates. Your 
									PDF file must not include paper id, page 
									number & conference name.

Travel Information
茫聙聙
General Information
Kobe is a beautiful port city in western Japan with a population of 1.5 million, located less than an
                hour by train from Osaka and Kyoto.
Kobe opened as Japan芒聙聶s first international trading port in 1868, and since then has developed into an
                international city that has adopted Western customs and culture.
Kobe tourism is characterized by its compact cityscape and cosmopolitan environment cultivated by its
                history.
The downtown area stretches approximately 2 km from north to south, between the sea and the mountains.
                Within this area, there are beautifully landscaped waterfront areas such as Meriken Park & Harbor
                Land, the Kitano area with its European-style houses, and Nankinmachi Chinatown, and many shopping and
                dining spots.
A short distance away, you can enjoy the rich nature of Mt. Rokko and Mt. Maya, Japan芒聙聶s largest sake
                production area (Nada Gogo), and one of the most famous hot spring resorts in Japan (Arima Onsen).
And of course, don芒聙聶t forget to try the world-famous Kobe Beef!茫聙聙
Access
>
                Access to Kobe is really simple and convenient. It is within an hour from Kyoto and Osaka, as well as
                the major regional airport, Kansai International Airport (KIX), and transport options include trains,
                buses, and also the Shinkansen (bullet train).
We recommend you to stay and take enough time to enjoy everything in Kobe, but transport access is so
                convenient that you can make a day trip from Osaka or Kyoto.
Access / Official Travel Guide of KOBE
(For more details about Kobe, please visit
https://www.feel-kobe.jp/en/access/
)

Kobe, Japan | July 11-13
2026 10th International Conference on Artificial 
						Intelligence and Virtual Reality
ABOUT AIVR
2026 10th International Conference on Artificial 
						Intelligence and Virtual Reality (AIVR2026)
brings together researchers and scientists, both 
						industrial and academic, developing novel Artificial 
						Intelligence and Virtual Reality outcomes. Research in 
						Virtual Reality (VR) is concerned with computing 
						technologies that allow humans to see, hear, talk, 
						think, learn, and solve problems in virtual and 
						augmented environments. Research in Artificial 
						Intelligence (AI) addresses technologies that allow 
						computing machines to mimic these same human abilities. 
						Although these two fields evolved separately, they share 
						an interest in human senses, skills, and knowledge 
						production. Thus, bringing them together will enable us 
						to create more natural and realistic virtual worlds and 
						develop better, more effective applications. Ultimately, 
						this will lead to a future in which interaction between 
						man and machine are made naturally in virtual worlds, 
						with use cases and benefits we are only just beginning 
						to imagine.
AIVR 2026 
						will be held in
Kobe, Japan
during
July 11-13, 2026
with focusing AI technologies and virtual reality. Past 
						conferences were held in Nagoya (2018), Singapore 
						(2019), virtual conferences (2020, 2021 and 2022), 
						Kumamoto (2023), Fukuoka (2024) and Osaka (2025) respectively. This 
						conference in the successful AIVR series provides an 
						ideal opportunity for reflection on developments over 
						the last two decades and to focus on future 
						developments. We look forward to welcoming AIVR2027 in 
						Kobe to provide the international community with a 
						highly interactive and scientifically excellent 
						conference to further the translation of promising AI & 
						VR technologies.
SPONSORED BY
SUPPORTED BY
CONFERENCE TRACKS
Track 1:Systems, including techniques, performance, and implementation
Track 2:Content creation and modelling
Track 3:AI technologies
Track 4:Cognitive aspects, perception, user behaviour
Track 5:Interactions / interactive and responsive environments
Track 6:Applications and use cases
KEY DATES
Deadline for Paper Submission | May 20, 2026
Notification of Acceptance | June 10, 2026
Deadline for Registration and Camera Ready 
							Submission | June 20, 2026
Dates of the Conference | July 11-13, 2026
SUBMISSSION
Each paper must be submitted via the
Online Submission System
after being prepared by following the Springer 
							Template Specific Template (
LaTeX 
							Template
or
Microsoft Word Template
) and be subject to both 
							review and editing.
Call for Papers (PDF)
PUBLICATION
All accepted papers will appear in the conference proceedings published by Springer-Verlag in One volume of
Springer Book Series Smart Innovation, System and Technologies (SIST)
.The book series is indexed by SCOPUS, EI Compendex, INSPEC, WTI Frankfurt eG, zbMATH, Japanese Science and Technology Agency (JST), SCImago, 
DBLP. All books published in the series are submitted for consideration in Web of Science.

Springer-SIST, Volume 463
| Indexed by EI Compendex

Springer-SIST, Volume 432
| Indexed by EI Compendex, Scopus

| Indexed by EI Compendex, Scopus

| Indexed by EI Compendex and Scopus.

AIVR2018 proceedings | Published by

| Indexed by EI Compendex and Scopus.
Extended 
and improved version of selected papers from AIVR2026 will be recommended to be 
published in
International 
Journal of Reasoning-based Intelligent Systems (ISSN online:1755-0564; ISSN 
print: 1755-0556)
, which will be submitted for indexing by Scopus 
(Elsevier), Compendex [formerly Ei] (Elsevier), Academic OneFile (Gale, 
cnpLINKer (CNPIEC), DBLP Computer Science Bibliography
AIVR 
							History
About Kobe
Kobe is a beautiful port city in western Japan with a population of 1.5 million, located less than an hour by train from Osaka and Kyoto. Kobe opened as Japan芒聙聶s first international trading port in 1868, and since then has developed into an international city that has adopted Western customs and culture.
Contact Us

aivr@chairmen.org
should you have any query about the conference.

Submission
Papers must be unpublished and must not 
									be submitted for publication elsewhere. All 
									papers will be reviewed by Program Committee 
									members and other experts active in the 
									field to ensure high quality and relevance 
									to the conference. Submissions should 
									include author information, abstract, 5-10 
									keywords, and be in PDF format. Paper length 
									should be longer than 10 pages in Springer 
									style format. It is imperative to prepare the paper 
									using the standard template (
LaTeX Template
or
Microsoft Word Template
).
Submission Method
Online submission system
or via email
aivr@chairmen.org
in PDF format.
All papers must explicitly describe their 
									relation to artificial intelligence, virtual 
									reality or their applications. All papers to 
									be submitted should be written in English 
									and formatted in the Springer-Verlag book 
									format. Please refer to the AIVR2026 and 
									Springer-Verlag web sites for directions on 
									the Paper Format which must be strictly 
									followed.
1. Each submission must be at least ten (10) 
									full pages. One regular registration can 
									cover a paper within Fifteen (15) pages, 
									including all figures, tables, and 
									references, Extra pages will be charged.
2. The full papers must be written in 
									English. All the tables and figures in the 
									paper should be original and un-published, 
									or authors must submit "Permission Request 
									Form"(
click to download
) for publication 
									purpose.聽 (If third-party materials are 
									used in your final paper, it is required by 
									Springer that authors should obtain 
									permission from third-party. Learn about 
									Third-Party Permissions:
https://www.springernature.com/gp/policies/editorial-policies/third-party-permissions
.
3. The full paper submission is required for 
									publication.
4. The version of your submission must be in 
									the requested format. The authors are 
									suggested to use the word template.
5. Please email aivr@chairmen.org if you 
									have any queries.
Plagiarism
Papers will be reviewed on the basis that 
									they do not contain plagiarized material and 
									have not been submitted to any other 
									conference at the same time (double 
									submission). These matters are taken very 
									seriously and the publisher will take action 
									against any author who has engaged in either 
									practice.
Review 
									process
AIVR welcomes the submission of application 
									papers with preference to the topics listed 
									in the call for papers. All submitted papers 
									will undergo a thorough review process; and 
									the peer review will be based on relevance, 
									originality, significance, quality and 
									clarity. AIVR follows a double-blind review 
									process. Authors are required to include 
									their names and affiliations in their papers 
									as illustrated in the sample templates. Your 
									PDF file must not include paper id, page 
									number & conference name.

Travel Information
茫聙聙
General Information
Kobe is a beautiful port city in western Japan with a population of 1.5 million, located less than an
                hour by train from Osaka and Kyoto.
Kobe opened as Japan芒聙聶s first international trading port in 1868, and since then has developed into an
                international city that has adopted Western customs and culture.
Kobe tourism is characterized by its compact cityscape and cosmopolitan environment cultivated by its
                history.
The downtown area stretches approximately 2 km from north to south, between the sea and the mountains.
                Within this area, there are beautifully landscaped waterfront areas such as Meriken Park & Harbor
                Land, the Kitano area with its European-style houses, and Nankinmachi Chinatown, and many shopping and
                dining spots.
A short distance away, you can enjoy the rich nature of Mt. Rokko and Mt. Maya, Japan芒聙聶s largest sake
                production area (Nada Gogo), and one of the most famous hot spring resorts in Japan (Arima Onsen).
And of course, don芒聙聶t forget to try the world-famous Kobe Beef!茫聙聙
Access
>
                Access to Kobe is really simple and convenient. It is within an hour from Kyoto and Osaka, as well as
                the major regional airport, Kansai International Airport (KIX), and transport options include trains,
                buses, and also the Shinkansen (bullet train).
We recommend you to stay and take enough time to enjoy everything in Kobe, but transport access is so
                convenient that you can make a day trip from Osaka or Kyoto.
Access / Official Travel Guide of KOBE
(For more details about Kobe, please visit
https://www.feel-kobe.jp/en/access/
)

AIVR 2026
Kobe, Japan | July 11-13
2026 10th International Conference on Artificial 
						Intelligence and Virtual Reality
ABOUT AIVR
2026 10th International Conference on Artificial 
						Intelligence and Virtual Reality (AIVR2026)
brings together researchers and scientists, both 
						industrial and academic, developing novel Artificial 
						Intelligence and Virtual Reality outcomes. Research in 
						Virtual Reality (VR) is concerned with computing 
						technologies that allow humans to see, hear, talk, 
						think, learn, and solve problems in virtual and 
						augmented environments. Research in Artificial 
						Intelligence (AI) addresses technologies that allow 
						computing machines to mimic these same human abilities. 
						Although these two fields evolved separately, they share 
						an interest in human senses, skills, and knowledge 
						production. Thus, bringing them together will enable us 
						to create more natural and realistic virtual worlds and 
						develop better, more effective applications. Ultimately, 
						this will lead to a future in which interaction between 
						man and machine are made naturally in virtual worlds, 
						with use cases and benefits we are only just beginning 
						to imagine.
AIVR 2026 
						will be held in
Kobe, Japan
during
July 11-13, 2026
with focusing AI technologies and virtual reality. Past 
						conferences were held in Nagoya (2018), Singapore 
						(2019), virtual conferences (2020, 2021 and 2022), 
						Kumamoto (2023), Fukuoka (2024) and Osaka (2025) respectively. This 
						conference in the successful AIVR series provides an 
						ideal opportunity for reflection on developments over 
						the last two decades and to focus on future 
						developments. We look forward to welcoming AIVR2027 in 
						Kobe to provide the international community with a 
						highly interactive and scientifically excellent 
						conference to further the translation of promising AI & 
						VR technologies.
SPONSORED BY
SUPPORTED BY
CONFERENCE TRACKS
Track 1:Systems, including techniques, performance, and implementation
Track 2:Content creation and modelling
Track 3:AI technologies
Track 4:Cognitive aspects, perception, user behaviour
Track 5:Interactions / interactive and responsive environments
Track 6:Applications and use cases
KEY DATES
Deadline for Paper Submission | May 20, 2026
Notification of Acceptance | June 10, 2026
Deadline for Registration and Camera Ready 
							Submission | June 20, 2026
Dates of the Conference | July 11-13, 2026

Each paper must be submitted via the
Online Submission System
after being prepared by following the Springer 
							Template Specific Template (
LaTeX 
							Template
or
Microsoft Word Template
) and be subject to both 
							review and editing.
Call for Papers (PDF)
PUBLICATION
All accepted papers will appear in the conference proceedings published by Springer-Verlag in One volume of
Springer Book Series Smart Innovation, System and Technologies (SIST)
.The book series is indexed by SCOPUS, EI Compendex, INSPEC, WTI Frankfurt eG, zbMATH, Japanese Science and Technology Agency (JST), SCImago, 
DBLP. All books published in the series are submitted for consideration in Web of Science.

Submission
Papers must be unpublished and must not 
									be submitted for publication elsewhere. All 
									papers will be reviewed by Program Committee 
									members and other experts active in the 
									field to ensure high quality and relevance 
									to the conference. Submissions should 
									include author information, abstract, 5-10 
									keywords, and be in PDF format. Paper length 
									should be longer than 10 pages in Springer 
									style format. It is imperative to prepare the paper 
									using the standard template (
LaTeX Template
or
Microsoft Word Template
).
Submission Method
Online submission system
or via email
aivr@chairmen.org
in PDF format.
All papers must explicitly describe their 
									relation to artificial intelligence, virtual 
									reality or their applications. All papers to 
									be submitted should be written in English 
									and formatted in the Springer-Verlag book 
									format. Please refer to the AIVR2026 and 
									Springer-Verlag web sites for directions on 
									the Paper Format which must be strictly 
									followed.
1. Each submission must be at least ten (10) 
									full pages. One regular registration can 
									cover a paper within Fifteen (15) pages, 
									including all figures, tables, and 
									references, Extra pages will be charged.
2. The full papers must be written in 
									English. All the tables and figures in the 
									paper should be original and un-published, 
									or authors must submit "Permission Request 
									Form"(
click to download
) for publication

.
3. The full paper submission is required for

4. The version of your submission must be in

5. Please email aivr@chairmen.org if you

Plagiarism
Papers will be reviewed on the basis that 
									they do not contain plagiarized material and 
									have not been submitted to any other 
									conference at the same time (double 
									submission). These matters are taken very 
									seriously and the publisher will take action 
									against any author who has engaged in either 
									practice.
Review 
									process
AIVR welcomes the submission of application 
									papers with preference to the topics listed 
									in the call for papers. All submitted papers 
									will undergo a thorough review process; and 
									the peer review will be based on relevance, 
									originality, significance, quality and 
									clarity. AIVR follows a double-blind review 
									process. Authors are required to include 
									their names and affiliations in their papers 
									as illustrated in the sample templates. Your 
									PDF file must not include paper id, page 
									number & conference name.

Travel Information
茫聙聙
General Information
Kobe is a beautiful port city in western Japan with a population of 1.5 million, located less than an
                hour by train from Osaka and Kyoto.
Kobe opened as Japan芒聙聶s first international trading port in 1868, and since then has developed into an
                international city that has adopted Western customs and culture.
Kobe tourism is characterized by its compact cityscape and cosmopolitan environment cultivated by its
                history.
The downtown area stretches approximately 2 km from north to south, between the sea and the mountains.
                Within this area, there are beautifully landscaped waterfront areas such as Meriken Park & Harbor
                Land, the Kitano area with its European-style houses, and Nankinmachi Chinatown, and many shopping and
                dining spots.
A short distance away, you can enjoy the rich nature of Mt. Rokko and Mt. Maya, Japan芒聙聶s largest sake
                production area (Nada Gogo), and one of the most famous hot spring resorts in Japan (Arima Onsen).
And of course, don芒聙聶t forget to try the world-famous Kobe Beef!茫聙聙
Access
>
                Access to Kobe is really simple and convenient. It is within an hour from Kyoto and Osaka, as well as
                the major regional airport, Kansai International Airport (KIX), and transport options include trains,
                buses, and also the Shinkansen (bullet train).
We recommend you to stay and take enough time to enjoy everything in Kobe, but transport access is so
                convenient that you can make a day trip from Osaka or Kyoto.
Access / Official Travel Guide of KOBE
(For more details about Kobe, please visit
https://www.feel-kobe.jp/en/access/
)
